etc.motd:
skivvy playground
  ──────────────────────────────────────
  integration testing for HTTP APIs
  tests are JSON · no code required

  · type 'tree' to explore the workspace
  · edit tests under tests/ with nano
  · edit mock api responses under api/ with nano
  · type 'skivvy --help' to see all options

comments.text: This happened to me in 2019 and I've been in witness protection ever since

Classic Steve

comments.text: My landlord once painted over a wasp nest and called it 'textured wall art'

replies.text: That's not a wall that's a biome
I would simply not live there
Rich your parents bought your condo please sit down

posts.title: I said 'you too' when the waiter said 'enjoy your meal' and I need to move cities

comments.text: I once said 'love you bye' to my dentist so at least you're not alone

replies.text: Did they say it back
They did not and Frank has switched dentists four times since

comments.text: I told a flight attendant 'you too' when she said 'have a safe flight' and I stared out the window for 6 hours straight

replies.text: The window stare of shame is universal
At least she IS flying too so technically you were correct
Tom that is not the point and you know it

It's true I wrote the convention

comments.text: I AM the roommate. It was salmon and it was delicious and I'd do it again

replies.text: GREG????
This is the crossover event of the century
Greg you monster
Tomorrow it's sardines

posts.title: My dog learned to open the fridge and honestly he's earned whatever's in there

comments.text: My dog figured out door handles and I've just accepted I live in his house now

replies.text: You pay rent to a dog?
In treats yes
That's a better deal than most landlords honestly

comments.text: Put a child lock on it. My toddler can't open the fridge so your dog probably can't either

replies.text: You underestimate this beagle. He has solved problems that would stump NASA engineers.
My beagle defeated a child lock in 11 minutes I timed it

metadata.motto: Come for the misery, stay because you're on hold

playground.README.md:
# Skivvy playground

Welcome — this browser playground is a self-contained sandbox where you can run and edit skivvy tests live.

This directory is also exposed in the repository at `examples/dev_server`.

## Quick start

```sh
ls
skivvy cfg.json
```

## Categories

Use the dropdown at the top of the page to switch between example sets, or run any config file directly:

| Config | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `cfg.json` | Basics — status codes, simple field checks |
| `cfg_diffs.json` | Failing tests with ndiff output |
| `cfg_diffs_unified.json` | Same failures, unified diff format |
| `cfg_diffs_table.json` | Same failures, side-by-side table format |
| `cfg_matchers.json` | All built-in matchers |
| `cfg_variables.json` | $store, $fetch, brace expansion, $write_file/$read_file |
| `cfg_oauth.json` | OAuth flow — POST login, capture token, use in header |
| `cfg_headers.json` | Sending request headers, asserting response headers |
| `cfg_graphql.json` | GraphQL-shaped responses, nested matching |

## Edit a test

```sh
nano tests/01_list_fortunes.json
skivvy cfg.json
```

## Edit the API response

```sh
nano api/fortunes/1.json
skivvy cfg.json
```

## Run it locally from the repo

```sh
cd examples/dev_server
uv run skivvy cfg.json
uv run skivvy cfg_matchers.json
uv run skivvy cfg_oauth.json
```

If you want to start the JSON server yourself:

```sh
cd examples/dev_server
python3 server.py 8080 api
```

The `skivvy` command uploads this workspace, starts the JSON server in the sandbox, runs the command, and streams the output back into the terminal.

01_response_diff.json._comment: Intentional response mismatch for diff demos

02_status_diff.json._comment: Intentional status mismatch for diff demos

01_query_users.json._comment: GraphQL responses wrap data in a 'data' object — test the structure and the array length

02_nested_fields.json._comment: match_subsets: check only the fields you care about, deep in a nested response

03_nested_author.json._comment: Deeply nested objects and array fields — author is an object, tags is an array

body.query: { posts { id title author { name } tags } }

04_no_errors.json._comment: Asserting 'errors' is null is a key GraphQL pattern — use $! to assert fields are absent

01_response_content_type.json._comment: Assert a specific response header using response_headers

02_send_custom.json._comment: Send custom request headers with read_headers — useful for API keys, versioning, tracing

03_auth_header.json._comment: Combine outgoing headers with response header assertions

03_string.json._comment: $contains substring check, $regexp pattern match, $text validates printable string

04_length_array.json._comment: $len checks the exact length of an array or string

06_membership.json._comment: $in checks the value is one of the listed options

07_validation.json._comment: $uuid validates a UUID, $valid_ip validates an IPv4 or IPv6 address

08_negation.json._comment: Prefix any matcher with $! to negate it

09_python_expr.json._comment: $expr evaluates a Python expression — 'actual' is bound to the value being checked

10_array_matching.json._comment: match_every_entry applies the pattern to ALL items. match_subsets checks only listed fields.

01_login.json._comment: POST credentials and capture the tokens — they become available to all later tests in this directory

02_profile.json._comment: Use the stored token in the Authorization header via brace expansion

03_refresh.json._comment: Send the refresh token in the request body - we don't verify the exact token we get back because it would make the test unnecessarily brittle

01_widget_listing.json._comment: Widget42 appears in the results — assert only what you care about

02_graphql_query.json._comment: Every post has a named author and at least one tag, comments have text and upvotes, no GraphQL errors

body.query: { posts { id title author { name } tags comments { text upvotes replies { text } } reactions } totalCount }

replies.text: $text

03_user_thumbnails.json._comment: Every user's thumbnail is a live, reachable URL — every image ID is unique

01_store.json._comment: $store captures a response value into a named variable for use in later tests

02_fetch_and_expand.json._comment: <var> in URLs expands stored variables. $fetch checks a value equals what was stored.

03_write_file.json._comment: $write_file saves a response value to disk so the next test can read it back

04_read_back.json._comment: $read_file reads a file written by a previous test and compares it to the response value

02_get_single_fortune.json._comment: Get a specific fortune by id
